desc:
The tunnel runs east to west, and passage is quite easy in either direction. The
walls are tall, and made of a smooth grey basalt rock. The floor is dusty, but
smooth and level. Strange noises can be heard echoing throughout the tunnel and
the direction of the sounds is indiscernible.
name: Wide Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel continues to the east or to the west, into the vast darkness in
either direction. Passage here is easy, with the floor being smooth, level, and
wide. The grey basalt walls rise high before curving into a smooth grey ceiling
above. Echoes of strange sounds can be heard coming from both directions.
name: Wide Tunnel

desc:
The long tunnel continues to the east and to the west here. The walls are made
of smooth, grey basalt rock that climb into the darkness above. The occasional
drop of water can be heard dripping from the smooth, curved ceiling above. The
air is stale and odourless, and strange sounds can be heard echoing all about.
name: Wide Tunnel

desc:
Three tunnels meet here, but the darkness makes it difficult to see where each
leads. A very slight breeze seems to be coming from the north, moving toward a
large opening to the west, and a dank, stagnant, musty smell emanates from
below.
name: Fog-filled Junction

desc:
Warm air from the north meets cooler air from the south, creating a surprisingly
strong wind through this narrow tunnel. As the wind passes around corners and
over irregularities in the wall, it whistles and howls in various pitches, and
from several locations, creating a confusing cacophony of sounds.
name: Windy Tunnel

desc:
A large opening from above narrows to a tiny entrance to the north. A foul smell
permeates the air, musty, and rank with decay. The slope upward is slick, the
smooth stone wet with condensation. Faint scratching sounds can be heard echoing
from the long, narrow tunnel to the north.
name: Narrow Entrance

desc:
Stripes and swirls of multicoloured, iridescent obsidian cover every surface of
this huge cavern. Condensation from the humid air glistens on the surface of the
walls, which further magnifies the dazzling effect of the polychromatic stone.
name: Variegated Cavern

desc:
Shards of razor-sharp, jet-black obsidian litter the ground. Large knife-like
outcroppings from the walls and ceiling create a treacherous environment.
Manoeuvring through this cave will require some skill, and it is unlikely that
one could make it through completely unharmed. A small opening has been cut into
the wall to the south.
name: Obsidian Cave

desc:
A small, broken table sits in the corner of this small cave. Bone, antler, and
wooden hammers of various sizes and shapes litter the floor, covered in dust.
Mounds of sharp obsidian scraps are piled around the table, and rough,
unfinished arrowheads, spearheads, and knives are scattered around.
name: Abandoned Workshop

desc:
The floor of this cave opens into a large crater that seems to have been formed
when the surface collapsed above a large void below. The edges of the pit curve
steeply downward, creating a treacherous situation for the unwary. Faint, nearly
imperceptible, sounds echo from below, indicating that someone, or something, is
moving around in the depths.
name: Pit Crater

desc:
This strange little nook has been cut neatly from the surrounding obsidian. The
walls, floor, and ceiling are perfectly square and plumb, forming a neat little
cubicle. It took master craftsmanship and substantial effort to create this tiny
space, but to what end the artist was working is utterly unclear.
name: Tiny Nook

desc:
A spiralling tunnel of black basalt was formed centuries ago by molten rock
flowing from a now extinct volcanic vent. Close examination reveals that this
chute was formed when the top, sides, and bottom of the flow cooled and
solidified, after which the insides of the lava river continued to flow,
emptying out the present cave. The surfaces of the surrounding rock are rippled,
giving the impression that the rock is still flowing freely.
name: Lava River Cave

desc:
Ripples of black basalt seem to flow out of the western wall of this cave, the
frozen remnants of a spring of molten rock. Heat emanates from the rock,
indicating that a new spring could form at any moment. There is a strong smell
of sulphur in the air.
name: Extinct Volcanic Vent

desc:
Dust and fragments of silk float heavily in the musty air. A tangle of cobwebs
extends to the east, continuing out of sight in that direction, in sharp
contrast to the smooth basalt formations in the rest of this cave. An opening in
the hard stone leads upward into total darkness.
name: Musty Entrance

desc:
A small hole leads down, barely large enough to allow passage to whatever lies
below. Sticky cobwebs hang from the ceiling and walls, choking off passageways
to the north and west. A thick, musty smell fills the air, making it difficult
to breathe.
name: Small Hole

desc:
Something has hollowed out the surrounding stone into a small, spherical
chamber. The ground is littered with human and animal bones, empty egg sacs, and
moulted spider skins. This appears to be a lair where young spiders are reared
until they grow to adulthood. Based on the size of several of the moulted skins,
the adult spiders that leave here are quite large.
name: Round Chamber

desc:
A long corridor extends to the west from this shallow recess. The only other
exit is a small opening in the ceiling. Long, thin strands of sticky web float
listlessly in the still air. The air has a dank, earthy smell, faintly
resembling rotting compost.
name: End of a Corridor

desc:
Several passages converge at a small chamber. The low ceiling makes it difficult
to manoeuvre, and there is some sticky substance on the floors and walls. There
are sounds coming from all directions, but they are muted by the thick, velvety
material covering the walls. The air is still, with a thick, musty odour.
name: Convergence

desc:
A large hole opens below, from which odious fumes emanate. Rustling sounds from
that direction echo and amplify off of the walls and ceiling. Venturing downward
seems imprudent. With some caution, it is possible to travel along the edges of
the hole without falling in.
name: Above a Sinister Burrow

desc:
Rocks and debris have fallen, apparently from a massive cave-in long ago. The
way is blocked in all directions except a small opening to the west. The thick
dust on the floor seems to indicate that this small space is seldom visited,
perhaps with good reason.
name: Blocked Passage

desc:
The ceiling extends dozens of feet above, as this tunnel opens into a large
grotto. Cobwebs stretch across every corner, spanning numerous stalactites
above. The rubble on the ground bears evidence to the instability of the
stalactites, making it a questionable choice to linger here below them.
name: Web-covered Grotto

desc:
Black walls with tiny specks of glistening crystals form a curving corridor.
Fine filaments float in the air, making it difficult to see, and the air is
stale and musty. Scratch marks on the floor suggest that creatures frequently
use this passage.
name: Bend

desc:
Tunnels from several directions converge here. The grey stone is strangely free
of the sticky threads that are prevalent in adjacent areas. The air is slightly
stale, but is free from any real odour. This place looks like it could be a
gathering or meeting place of some sort, which might explain the lack of any
encumbrances.
name: Converging Tunnels

desc:
Empty cocoons, torn clothing, broken and rusted weapons, piles of bones, and
other inedible trash lie in piles all over the floor of this room. A thick
reddish-brown substance, likely coagulating blood, covers the floor. Lingering
would likely result in becoming a meal for whatever seems to regularly dine
here.
name: Feeding Room

desc:
A deep depression has been burrowed in the ground, clearly home to some
creature, or possibly a whole family of them. Countless bones and skulls litter
the floor. Whatever lives here clearly has a voracious appetite. The floor and
walls are covered with a foul-smelling, mud-like substance, possibly excrement
or other body fluid from the creatures that dwell here.
name: Sinister Burrow

desc:
A long, narrow corridor runs east-west here. The air is cool and damp, with a
light, foul-smelling breeze coming from the west. There are several large
cavities along the floor, some possibly large enough to enter, and most covered
with intricate webs of sticky silk.
name: Damp Corridor

desc:
Warm, damp air is flowing steadily from the west with a peculiar odour, a
combination of brimstone, decaying flesh, and rotting plants. The walls are wet,
and condensation drips constantly from above. Short, fine, filaments float in
the air, making it difficult to breathe.
name: Damp Corridor

desc:
There is little room to move in this narrow pit. Sticky strands of silk cling to
everything, and the floor slopes steeply toward the centre, making it difficult
to get out, as if this hole were intentionally constructed as some sort of trap.
name: Web-covered Hole

desc:
A portion of the floor of the corridor above has collapsed, creating a deep
sinkhole. Jagged rocks protrude from the floor and walls, making movement
treacherous. Water drips constantly from above, collecting in the cracks and
crevices in the floor.
name: Sinkhole

desc:
A passage from the east ends abruptly here. A large hole below is spanned by
several webs, waiting to catch anything that falls down from here. Long strands
of thin silk hang from above, in varying shades of grey and white, reaching out
to cling to anything passing through. Every nook and cranny in this cave is
choked with cobwebs.
name: Above a Web-covered Hole

desc:
Layers upon layers of delicate, spiralling webs completely cover every opening.
It is questionable whether travel in any direction is prudent, though staying
here seems like a bad idea as well. Cocoons, some of them with seemingly live
occupants, hang from several of the webs, confirming that this is not a place to
linger.
name: Choked Tunnel

desc:
An east-west tunnel narrows here, creating a natural bottleneck. Several spider
webs cross the passageway, ready to snare anything that ventures into this
passageway. Several webs have been broken, and contain large cocoons of silk.
Frantic movement can be seen inside several of the cocoons, indicating that
whatever, or whoever, got caught in the web was cocooned alive.
name: Clogged Bottleneck

desc:
Thick, sticky cobwebs cover every surface, making movement difficult. Strands of
web float through the air. Openings to the east, west, and south can barely be
seen through the tangle of webs covering them. The webs vibrate and shift every
now and again.
name: Sticky Passage

desc:
This tunnel is completely covered in sticky webs. Strands of grey and white
thread have been woven into intricate, spiralling patterns spanning the entire
width of the cave in several places. The passage to the east is completely
blocked with an impregnable mass of overlapping woven webs, hardened with time
to form a solid wall that rivals the surrounding stone in durability. It appears
that there may be an exit to the west, though making it past several intervening
webs without getting trapped might prove to be challenging.
name: Web-filled Tunnel

desc:
A tunnel branches into several directions here. The passageway to the north
seems to open into a larger area, though it is so covered with cobwebs, it is
difficult to be sure what lies beyond. A dark tunnel leads to the west, and the
air from that direction is rank. There is also an opening above leading to a
small cave.
name: Branching Tunnel

desc:
Broken crates, chests, and boxes are scattered around here, along with shelves
filled with broken jars and other abandoned supplies. Several barrels are lined
up against the wall. Thick dust covers every surface, indicating that this place
has not been used for decades.
name: Abandoned Storage Area

desc:
Black basalt forms a tunnel running east-west. The tunnel is nearly perfectly
cylindrical, though whether formed naturally or by some intelligent workforce is
impossible to discern. Scratching sounds echo throughout this space, seemingly
coming from every direction.
name: Basalt Tunnel

desc:
A steep chute leads down to a refuse pit to the south, from which a strong,
foetid smell emanates. An opening above appears to be a vent shaft, though the
stench would indicate that vent is ineffective. Thick clumps of cobwebs float
through the air and cling to the walls, and sounds of movement can be heard
echoing from the east.
name: Trash Chute

desc:
Trash, bones, broken tools, carcasses in various states of decay, and other
detritus are piled in rotting mounds all around this chamber. A thick fluid,
sticky and foul smelling, oozes out from under several of the mounds, slowly
seeping along a slight declivity towards a pit to the west. A steep chute slopes
steeply upwards to the north, and there appears to be a small opening to the
south.
name: Refuse Pit

desc:
This narrow shaft appears to have been formed as a vent from the area below.
However, it appears that a rockslide, possibly caused by an earthquake, has
blocked the opening above with huge boulders. Based on the thick grime covering
the rocks, this vent has been blocked for quite some time.
name: Blocked Vent

desc:
A small indentation in the rock has created this alcove. The foul smells from
the garbage pile to the north mix with stale, stagnant air here, creating an
entirely unwelcoming environment. Every surface of this cave is teaming with
insects or other small creatures.
name: Small Alcove

desc:
The floor is littered with bones and other debris. A thick, brown, sticky
substance covers every surface and gives off a strong, foetid smell. There is a
large hole leading upwards into the darkness. Wisps of thin strands float in in
the air, clinging to everything they touch.
name: Bottom of a Pit

desc:
The narrow tunnel from the south ends abruptly here at a large hole leading to
darkness below. Sticky strands of fibre float in the air and cover every
surface. Fine, hairlike filaments crisscross the opening, glistening with fine
droplets of condensation from the humid air.
name: Precipitous Pit

desc:
Water drips from the low ceiling, pooling on the floor in shallow puddles. A
faint rustling can be heard from the north, echoing through this narrow space.
To the south, there appears to be an opening into a larger space, while the
passage to the north continues to narrow.
name: Cramped Tunnel

desc:
There are rocks and rubble littering the ground, clearly the result of a massive
cave-in long ago. The air here is stagnant, wet, and putrid. Rocks and debris
block the way in nearly every direction, but there is a way out to the west.
name: Cave-in

desc:
The ledge is wide and passage is easier here than it is in other areas along it.
To the west, the ceiling begins to slope downward as the ledge narrows. To the
east, the ledge widens even more and the ceiling opens up. The walls here are
smooth and made of columnar stone. There is a crack on the southern wall, where
some black liquid has run down, dried, and left a stain.
name: Narrow Ledge above a Dark Lake

desc:
The ledge here is nice and wide, making passage comfortable and easy. The
ceiling is high and large stalactites hang in the vast darkness above. Splashing
can be heard from the lake to the north, far below the ledge. A large gaping
crack can be seen in the wall to the south, stained black from some dried liquid
that once ran down the crack's surface. Natural grey stone steps lead upward to
the east, as the path continues along the ledge.
name: Narrow Ledge above a Dark Lake

desc:
The ledge opens up and becomes large, flat, and wide in the corner of the vast
cavern here above the large underground lake. It continues to wind to the west,
where some natural stone steps lead downward into the darkness. To the north,
the ledge slopes downward, making this the highest point of the entire path. The
dark surface of the lake sits about thirty feet below. The large stalactites
that hang above the lake are now eye level.
name: Wide Ledge around a Dark Lake

desc:
A large boulder sits against the wall to the east, partially obstructing the
ledge and making passage more difficult. To the south, the ledge slopes upward
toward the corner of the cavern. Beyond the boulder to the north, the ledge
narrows significantly, making travel in that direction slightly more difficult.
The smooth, grey, columnar walls are dry and cold to the touch.
name: Narrow Ledge above a Dark Lake

desc:
The ledge slopes down from the south, where it starts to become even with the
rest of the cavern floor. The ledge is narrow, and the surface of the lake
becomes closer as the ledge makes its descent northward. The huge stalactites
that hang from the ceiling above appear small at first, but become larger as the
ceiling slopes down toward the north and becomes lower. Air seems to be flowing
in from that direction, and carries a foul, putrid odour with it.
name: Narrow Ledge above a Dark Lake

desc:
The ceiling here becomes low, with large stalactites protruding down, making
passage to the north seemingly difficult. To the south, the floor slopes upward
into the darkness and forms a ledge over a large, underground lake. Faint
splashing can be heard in the distance.
name: Low Roofed Cavern

desc:
The cavern narrows significantly into a tight, cramped tunnel that curves to the
south and east. There are both stalactites that hang from the ceiling as well as
stalagmites that jut up from the ground that densely fill the cavern. While
travel in either direction is still possible, the obstacles make it much more
difficult.
name: Narrow Twisting Tunnel

desc:
The ceiling of the caverns slant down from the south and east and come to a
corner here. There are small stalactites lining the ceiling, hanging down like
spikes in the darkness. The floor of the cavern is wet and muddy, and small
bones are scattered about. Green and black fungi grows on a giant boulder to the
north and the western wall.
name: Wet Corner of a Dark Cavern

desc:
Huge stalactites, as large around as full-grown mountain trolls, hang here in
the vast darkness of the cavern. A foul, black liquid covers them and
occasionally drips off into the lake below. The surface of the lake is smooth
and calm, with the exception of the occasional drip from the stalactites above.
name: Stagnant Underground Lake

desc:
A pair of large, symmetrical stalactites hang from the ceiling above. They are
almost identical, as if created here intentionally. They resemble the fangs of
some large beast, dripping with a black liquid that looks like some foul venom.
To the east and south, steep grey columnar stone walls rise from the water.
name: Stagnant Underground Lake

desc:
Giant stalactites protrude from the ceiling above, covered in black liquid that
drips into a smooth, calm lake below. The steep grey walls rise up from the
edges of the lake, and plateau off at the top at what appears to be a ledge.
name: Stagnant Underground Lake

desc:
Large stalactites hang like chandeliers from the darkness above. They glisten
with moisture when the faintest hint of light touches them, and black liquid
drips steadily from them into a large, black lake below. The surface of the
water is smooth as glass, save the occasional drip from the ceiling or splash of
some fish from within.
name: Stagnant Underground Lake

desc:
The roof of the cavern is high and covered with stalactites that hang down like
spikes in the vast darkness. They are covered by a thick, black liquid that
occasionally drips down the ground below. The air here is thick, heavy, wet, and
musty. A foul odour emanates from the east, while the roof slopes down to the
west and the stalactites become thick and dense.
name: Open Cavern

desc:
This is a large, open cavern with huge stalactites hanging from the ceiling. All
about are large, grey boulders. Green and black mosses grow on the wall to the
south and line the tops of the boulders as well. The air here is damp and musty.
name: Open Cavern

desc:
The ceiling of the cavern slopes down to this corner from the north and west.
There are large boulders all about, with green and black mosses lining the tops
of them. The walls to the east and south are also lined with moss. The cavern
seems to open up to the west and north, while faint dripping sounds can be heard
echoing off the cavern walls.
name: Corner of a Cavern

desc:
The cavern here is huge and wide open, with boulders strewn about here and
there. Thin strands of green and black moss line the tops of the boulders, while
thin grey strands of some sort of cave fungi hang from the ceiling. On the
ground, tiny bones and fish skulls can be found scattered about the cavern
floor.
name: Dark Open Cavern

desc:
Slimy, multi-coloured fungi line the northern wall and roof of the cavern.
Musty, damp air can be detected coming in from the east. It carries with it the
stench of death and decay. The floor is damp and slightly muddy, and there is a
large, gaping crack in the stone floor below.
name: Dark Cavern

desc:
The ceiling is densely covered with short, sharp stalactites hanging down over
the dark lake below, and dripping with a thick, black liquid. The occasional
drop falls onto the surface of the lake. The bases of the stalactites at the
ceiling are surrounded by clusters of multi-coloured fungi.
name: Dark Underground Lake

desc:
The ceiling is high above the lake below and covered in fungi, decaying and
dripping with black liquid. A foul odour fills the air. From the lake, large
grey basalt columns rise high and connect with the ceiling. The black liquid
dripping down covers them, and they glisten when light touches them.
name: Dark Underground Lake

desc:
The ceiling here is covered with thick clusters of fungi, dripping with a thick,
black liquid, and rising high above the lake below. Slimy, multi- coloured fungi
cover the wall to the north. The wall to the west is smooth, grey basalt, but
the thick, black liquid from the ceiling runs down it toward the lake below.
Higher up on the western wall, there appears to be a crawlspace that can be
reached by climbing up to it.
name: Dark Underground Lake

desc:
There is a wide rock ledge that juts out over the dark lake below it. The ledge
is slimy and slippery and a foul odour emanates up to it from the lake below.
Slimy fungi cover the wall to the north, dripping and oozing with a thick black
liquid that runs down and seemingly feeds the lake.
name: Damp Dark Cavern

desc:
The crawlspace is small and cramped, but provides a safe area from the dark lake
below. Clusters of slimy, multi-coloured fungi line the walls and ceiling here.
Some of these fungi ooze with the black liquid that drips from the ceiling. The
small space makes the disgusting odour that fills this area more concentrated,
which makes breathing difficult.
name: Narrow Crawlspace

desc:
The ceiling of the cavern slopes drastically to the east and north here.
Stalactites hang down low, some almost touching the floor. Thin strands of some
type of strange grey cave fungus hang freely from the ceiling, and also run down
the stalactites and walls.
name: Dark Cavern

desc:
The cavern walls to the south and east converge here into a corner. Green and
black mosses and fungi grow on the walls and on boulders that are strewn about.
The air is damp and musty, and air flow can be felt coming from the north. Small
stalactites hang from the ceiling here.
name: Corner of a Dark Cavern

desc:
There is a large, gaping crack in the northern cave wall, and a slight breeze
can be felt coming through. A dank odour fills the cave, while dead and decaying
roots line every wall. The ceiling is low and makes the space cramped, but it
suddenly ascends to the north before it stops suddenly when it meets the steep,
grey stone wall.
name: Musty Cavern Corner

desc:
Hidden behind a huge boulder, the corner of this cave is surprisingly large. A
disgusting smell fills the cave here, and the walls are lined with dead and
decaying roots and vegetation. Salamanders and geckos scurry all about, trying
to hide in the vegetation and stay out of sight.
name: Hidden Corner

desc:
The walls of this natural indentation in the rock glisten with condensation. A
thick, dank draught comes from above, and the air is cold and wet. Every now and
then, faint splashing sounds can be heard from above. As there is no evidence
that anyone or anything has travelled through here, it seems that this area is
avoided by visitors, perhaps with good reason.
name: Damp Recess

desc:
Thick, wet mud covers the floor of this shallow depression, at times nearly
knee-deep. The walls and ceiling are covered with green-blue slime that gives of
a stale, musty odour. While it is difficult to be sure, there appears to be
something moving beneath the surface.
name: Muddy Depression

desc:
Dry stone gives way to hard-packed, damp earth as this cramped channel extends
from east to west. The walls form a wedge as they taper inward and nearly come
together at the floor, leaving only a thin strip on which to walk. Passage to
the west becomes more difficult, while there appears to be an easier egress to
the east.
name: Narrow Ditch

desc:
Solid stone has been carved away to form a small chamber. The walls and ceiling
are only roughly formed, and mining tools litter the ground. It looks as if work
in this mine shaft was abruptly interrupted for some reason, and all work
abandoned.
name: Roughly Hewn Chamber

desc:
A crude shaft has been dug here, apparently the early phase of a mining
operation. Heavy, square timbers support the walls and ceiling at regular
intervals, giving off a heavy smell of pine tar. The splintered, sagging wooden
supports have decayed with time, never meant to be more than temporary.
name: Crude Mineshaft

desc:
Sections of the tunnel walls have been chipped away by crude tools. There are
short, dead-end tunnels in several directions, as if someone was exploring the
surrounding earth for possible valuable ore. A mineshaft extends to the south,
while natural openings lead to the north and west.
name: Entrance to a Mine

desc:
Veins of glittering ore run through the stone, streaking across the tunnel in a
stark contrast with the surrounding dull black walls. The ore glistens with
bright silver, gold, and white glints, even in the darkness, and the floor is
covered with sparkling dust.
name: Glittering Tunnel

desc:
Strange whispers and clicking noises echo in the deep. The tunnel walls are
infested with strange insects that crawl over the walls on many legs, appearing
and quickly disappearing in the tiny burrows in the black stone. The floor is
covered in fecal matter and black, empty carapaces.
name: Infested Tunnel

desc:
Inky darkness permeates the surrounding area, making it difficult to discern
space, distance, and even time. While the blackness creates a sombre,
unwelcoming atmosphere, there is a sense that the areas to the north and south
may be even more disquieting.
name: Dark Tunnel

desc:
The emptiness of this pitch black tunnel is palpable and unwelcoming. Travel
would be perilous were it not for the smooth, even floor. It appears that this
tunnel was created to provide a passageway for travel, though why anyone would
willingly travel here is much less apparent.
name: Dark Tunnel

desc:
It is profoundly dark and silent here, as if the very walls are swallowing every
ounce of energy. The utter emptiness is reminiscent of a tomb, and could make
someone entering here question whether they are still among the living. The lack
of any sensory input makes it difficult to distinguish which direction this
tunnel leads.
name: Noiseless Tunnel

desc:
Cold, hard walls form a tunnel cut from solid stone long ago, sloping upward
from north to south. It is cool and dry here, and the air is eerily still. The
centre of the floor is smooth, as if worn by many years of foot traffic. The
tunnel disappears into utter darkness to the north.
name: Sloping Tunnel

desc:
The air in this tunnel is still, and strangely free of any odour. The passageway
has been carved from solid rock. While tool marks can be observed here and
there, the walls are mostly smooth, apparently worn with age. The floor is even,
but clearly slopes upward to the south.
name: Sloping Tunnel

desc:
A carved tunnel leading north ends abruptly at a small opening to the south. It
is profoundly dark, quiet, and strangely unsettling here. It cannot be
determined how far this tunnel extends to the north, or what is beyond the
opening to the south.
name: End of a Tunnel

desc:
A stone stair coming from above ends at this rough landing. There are broken
bones and other debris scattered all over the ground, clearly the result of
various mishaps on the stairs above. To the north, a long passagway leads into
the still darkness.
name: Bottom of a Stair

desc:
Other than the steep steps in the stone, this narrow vertical channel is
remarkably featureless. From here, it seems that these stairs continue upward
indefinitely, but there is a sense that the bottom is not far below. The silence
of this place is profound, combining with the darkness and an unnatural chill in
the air to create an environment that causes even the most hardy and fearless to
take pause.
name: Steep Stone Stairs

desc:
Steep stairs extend up and down from here, seemingly forever in both directions.
The stairs are steep, with exceedingly narrow treads. The slightest misstep
would result in finding the bottom rather more quickly than would be safe.
name: Steep Stone Stairs

desc:
Grey stone stairs, seemingly natural, lead up to a large opening and down into
the vast darkness below. The natural stairs are made of gritty grey basalt,
creating enough grip for a firm footing and making the passage up and down easy
to navigate.
name: Natural Stone Stairs

desc:
Bones of all shapes and sizes litter the ground and hang in the sticky web that
covers this whole pit. Egg sacs are everywhere. The different sizes, shapes, and
colours of the sacs indicate that this is the lair of several different mother
spiders.
name: Lair

desc:
Thick strands of sticky silk span this cave at random intervals and angles from
walls, floor, and ceiling, creating a haphazard tangle of intersecting threads.
The web is so thick that it nearly chokes off the entire passageway. Bones,
empty clothing, and other remnants of living things hang tangled here and there
in the sticky web.
name: Tangled Cave

desc:
Long strands of sticky webs hang from the walls and ceiling, seemingly covering
every stone, and so thick in places that they could be mistaken for fog. Some
strands are as hard as iron while others are soft and flexible, the combination
creating the opportunity for an effective, and likely lethal, trap.
name: Web-filled Tunnel

desc:
Cramped and dark, this small hollow opens into a wide tunnel to the south. The
sloping floor and low ceiling make this an inviting space in which to linger.
The air is musty and thick with dust and fine, silky filaments. The opening to
the south is obscured by thick tangles of cobwebs.
name: Cramped Cavity

desc:
A thick web, made up of silk strands of various sizes, from thin and sticky to
thick and iron-strong, nearly blocks this entire vertical passage. Careful
manoeuvring will be required to pass through these webs without becoming
entangled.
name: Web-choked Chute

desc:
A shift in the earth centuries ago has formed a narrow ledge that slopes steeply
upward from the cave below and continues to the east. The hard stone ends in a
precipitous edge leading into an abyss of darkness below. Travel along this
ledge is possible for the sure of foot.
name: Narrow Ledge

desc:
This narrow ledge extends east-west, high above the cave floor below. While the
width here is barely wide enough to allow a tenuous purchase, the ledge narrows
sharply to the east, making travel in that direction impossible. Sand and rubble
scattered across the surface prevent sure footing, making a slip and potentially
deadly fall a distinct possibility.
name: Narrow Ledge

desc:
Nearly vertical, this narrow chute is choked with thick tangles of cobwebs.
Strong, heavy silk strands, the size of sturdy rope, criss-cross the opening at
irregular intervals creating hand- and footholds that make it possible to move
through this otherwise impassable tunnel.
name: Web-choked Chute

desc:
The warm, humid air from the lake wafts in from the south through the cracks and
voids around the giant boulder concealing the entrance to this hidden cavern.
The floor is damp and muddy, and the bottoms of the grey stone wall are wet as
well. The wetness of the walls gradually disappears as it climbs up, and the top
portions of the wall are lighter grey. A thick blanket of green and black fungi
grows on the floor around the base of the walls, climbing and spreading up the
wetness. A thick, black liquid slowly drips down from some cracks in the western
wall, and forms a small puddle.
name: Hidden Cavern
